best: Suggesting the maven-test use MockWebServer as a normal test is a vague 'make it a real test' nudge, not a non-obvious test insight.
Points anchored on the test file are really about the AsyncDns callback API design (multiple onAddressResults vs CompletableFuture), not test correctness or coverage.
Questioning whether the right fix is changing the URL rather than flipping the expected assertion from 200 to 404 touches test-correctness, but the reviewer immediately concedes 'this is fine.'
none (only an import-order nit on the test, praise, and a question about clone() in production code).
Notes that testTrinoReadingSparkCompressedData's SET statements are now no-ops, so the product test no longer exercises what it claims and may be pointless to keep.
none (only 'why is this required'/'why a new module' questions about test scaffolding, no concrete test insight).
Asks for a test that verifies no vector type/function contributors are missing (mirroring the spatial ClassNamesTest), catching a real coverage gap where the contributor lists could silently drift.
none (points concern ArtifactKey semantics and BuildToolHelper API placement, not the tests).
none (the substantive point is a Maven test-scope/testbom dependency-management debate, not review of unit-test logic or coverage).
none (only 'this can be removed' on duplicate pom lines).
Spots that the refactored runner now changes the AssumptionViolatedException path (previously super.run ran; the new control flow gates it on setupException) and asks about JUnit-runner side effects.
Requests copyTo tests where end <= start; partially overlaps the existing testCopyToArgsReversed/testCopyToNothing but flags an edge-case coverage boundary worth nailing down.
